title: mRemoteNG config xml stored and opened from OneDrive, OneDrive stuck syncing xml file
author: ivassallo19
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
So I started saving and opening the config xml from my OneDrive for Business local location, C:\Users\user\OneDrive - company.com.au\mRemoteNG-name.xml, and now OneDrive repeatedly gets stuck syncing that file. 0 KB out of 346 KB.
I created a subfolder C:\Users\user\OneDrive - company.com.au\mRemote\mRemoteNG-name.xml, and opened it from there, but it still happens.
If I close OneDrive and reopen, it processes and syncs files...

iterations.description: OneDrive's file syncing mechanism can cause file locking, preventing mRemoteNG from saving or loading its configuration XML, leading to data loss or inaccessible configurations.

comments.author: jmiller76
comments.snippet:
Right click on the file and Choose "Always Keep on this Device".
It will still sync this way..  The issue is that it keeps removing the local copy and having to pull it again.

comments.author: ivassallo19
comments.snippet:
> Right click on the file and Choose "Always Keep on this Device".
> It will still sync this way.. The issue is that it keeps removing the local copy and having to pull it again.

Thank you. I forgot / overlooked to include this information. I currently have this file set as 'Always keep on this device'.
However it continues to occur.

What I have discovered is that mRemoteNG updates this file each time a connection is opened or closed. I am going to monitor this further and see if multipl...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): OneDrive's file syncing mechanism can cause file locking, preventing mRemoteNG from saving or loading its configuration XML, leading to data loss or inaccessible configurations.
Approach: Implement a more robust file access mechanism in XmlConnectionsSaver and XmlConnectionsLoader, potentially involving retries with backoff, shared file access modes, or temporary file writes followed by atomic renames to avoid file locking conflicts with cloud synchronization services like OneDrive.
